D1.justification: Every claim (C01-C10) is a descriptive/improvement-type treatment-effect statement grounded in an internal randomized placebo arm with exact table/figure numbers and verbatim source quotes, which is the right evidence type for RCT re-analysis claims.
D1.weakness: C09 (long-term durability, 154 weeks) is supported only by a non-randomized, propensity-weighted external control (ADNI) rather than a concurrent comparator, which is a materially weaker evidence class for a causal-durability claim even though the limitation is stated elsewhere.

D2.justification: Each claim carries an explicit, checkable falsification criterion tied to a CI crossing zero/one or a stated p-value threshold, and criteria are scope-matched to what was actually tested.
D2.weakness: Several falsification criteria (e.g. C01/C02/C03) are just a restatement of 'CI includes the null,' which is standard significance-testing logic rather than a genuinely risky, non-trivial prediction that could distinguish this analysis's insight from a null result.

D3.justification: Claims closely track the reported statistics and the ARA explicitly foregrounds the post-hoc, exploratory, non-multiplicity-corrected nature of the whole analysis plus eight named limitations (L1-L8), showing strong calibration discipline.
D3.weakness: Claims are uniformly marked 'supported' with no differential hedging despite the paper itself warning that none of the ten comparisons is multiplicity-corrected, so claim strength does not visibly scale with how exploratory/secondary each specific endpoint is (e.g. CDR-SB domain-level C04 vs primary C02).

D4.justification: The chain from observations (O1-O4: EU label restriction, ε4/ARIA dose-relationship, EMA imputation mandate, trial not isolating the subgroup) to gaps (G1-G2) to the key insight to the eight analysis plans (E01-E08) to claims (C01-C10) to evidence tables is fully traceable with no contradictions across layers.
D4.weakness: The insight statement is essentially a restatement of the four observations rather than a genuinely generative synthesis, so the 'insight' node adds little beyond summarizing the motivating gaps.

D5.justification: The exploration tree records two specific, genuine dead ends (N11: EU-eligible LTE control infeasible because ADNI lacks siderosis/anticoagulant/BP data; N12: hybrid imputation incompatible with propensity reweighting), which are concrete and honestly reported.
D5.weakness: Outside those two feasibility dead ends, every other node is 'explicit/decision or experiment' with no recorded alternative that was seriously considered and rejected — the tree reads as a re-narration of the published methods section rather than a trace of real deliberation over competing analytic choices.

D6.justification: Methods are appropriately matched to endpoint type (MMRM for continuous scales, Cox PH for progression, generalized mixed model for stabilisation proportions, Wilson CIs for clearance rates) and reported with full CIs and p-values.
D6.weakness: No multiplicity adjustment across ten claims/endpoints is applied or even approximated despite being flagged as a known limitation, and the central safety comparison (32.0% vs 36.8% any-ARIA between EU-eligible and overall population) is presented descriptively with no confidence interval or formal test, undermining the rigor of C08's core claim.

findings.text: No multiplicity correction is applied across the ten claims/endpoints despite this being an explicitly acknowledged limitation (L1); every claim is nonetheless labeled 'supported' with uniform confidence, so the ARA does not visibly discount claim strength for the known false-positive inflation risk of a post-hoc, exploratory, multi-endpoint analysis.

C09's long-term durability/early-vs-delayed-start claim rests on a non-randomized propensity-weighted external control (ADNI) rather than a concurrent randomized comparator, and 74.8% of the 'early-start' group received placebo for the entire LTE — a materially weaker evidence base for a causal claim than the RCT-internal claims C01-C08, even though the limitation is documented in constraints.md.

The exploration trace's only two dead ends are narrow implementation-feasibility constraints (ADNI missing covariates, imputation/reweighting incompatibility); no other node documents a rejected alternative analytic choice, so the trace functions more as a narrated methods section than genuine evidence of deliberated research decisions.
